user_query:
Read input/brief.md, input/keywords.csv, input/schedule.yaml, and input/constraints.json. Using that context, create an editorial package for THREE blog posts that moves each post through a complete content workflow (draft, outline, headline options, an editing note, SEO optimization details, a publication schedule, hashtags, social hooks, a call-to-action, tone, a rewrite note, translation targets, and simple stats). Produce the following files:

1) output/posts/post_1.json, output/posts/post_2.json, and output/posts/post_3.json — each must be a JSON object with these fields and constraints:
- draft: string (a brief draft idea)
- outline: array of at least 4 strings
- headlines: array of at least 5 unique strings; at least one headline must include the post’s optimize.target_keyword as a substring (case-insensitive)
- edit: string (an editing note)
- optimize: object with keys { target_keyword: string, keyword_density: number, meta_description: string }. keyword_density must be numeric; meta_description must be 160 characters or fewer
- schedule: ISO 8601 UTC time string ending with “Z” (e.g., 2026-05-06T09:00:00Z). Scheduled times must be unique across the three posts
- hashtags: array of at least 8 unique strings; every item must start with “#” and the set must include “#serverless”
- hooks: array of at least 2 strings; at least one must contain a digit (0-9)
- cta: string that contains one of these words (case-insensitive): “guide”, “checklist”, or “report”
- tone: string that mentions “authoritative” or “approachable” (case-insensitive)
- rewrite: string (briefly describe a major revision that was made or planned)
- translate: array that must include "es" and "de"
- stats: object with keys { headline_count: number, hashtag_count: number } that exactly match the counts of headlines and hashtags for that post
- status: string equal to "ready"

2) output/summary.csv — a CSV with header exactly: post_id,target_keyword,scheduled_date,headline_choice,hashtags_count and 3 data rows (one per post). Each row must:
- post_id be 1, 2, or 3
- target_keyword match optimize.target_keyword from the corresponding post JSON
- scheduled_date match schedule from the corresponding post JSON
- headline_choice be exactly one of the headlines for that post
- hashtags_count equal the number of hashtags for that post

3) output/activity.jsonl — at least 12 lines; each line a JSON object with keys { type: string, post_id: number, message: string }. Valid types are: draft, outline, headline, edit, optimize, schedule, hashtags, hooks, cta, tone, rewrite, translate. Every type must appear at least once across the file, and each post_id (1, 2, 3) must have at least 6 entries total across any types.

Make sure schedules are unique across the three posts, all JSON is valid, and the CSV header and columns match exactly. Write only to the specified output/ paths.
